sonnet_review.biasedText: So, I'm unwell. In the best way possible.
sonnet_review.reason: Hyperbolic, emotionally charged phrasing that dramatizes excitement about a discount; defensible as sensational tone.

sonnet_review.biasedText: They say scent sparks emotion. But for deal-hungry shoppers, a steep discount delivers its own kind of euphoria.
sonnet_review.reason: Characterizes shoppers and reactions with interpretive, emotive language (“deal-hungry,” “euphoria”); a reasonable spin/sensationalism call.
“Perfect” is an unqualified value judgment in the author’s voice; reasonable to flag as subjective marketing language.
“Beloved” is subjective praise presented as fact, typical of promotional tone; reasonable to flag.

gpt_review.biasedText: But for deal-hungry shoppers, a steep discount delivers its own kind of euphoria.
gpt_review.reason: Uses emotive exaggeration (“euphoria”) to describe discounts; defensible as sensational tone.

gpt_review.biasedText: You were smart and planned ahead, shopped early, and already grabbed the perfect candle to set the mood.
gpt_review.reason: Calls early shoppers “smart” and the candle “perfect,” presenting subjective value judgments as if generally true.

gpt_review.biasedText: So, I'm unwell. In the best way possible.
gpt_review.reason: Hyperbolic first-person excitement adds interpretive spin rather than neutral description.

gpt_review.biasedText: Thousands of reviewers swoon over these candles
gpt_review.reason: Author’s verb choice (“swoon”) adds enthusiastic framing to attributed reviews; reasonable to flag as spin/loaded language.
